action.description: Harborline Mechanical - emergency chiller repair invoice approval

email_thread.subject: Review packet for HMS-2026-0816
email_thread.body: Please review HMS-2026-0816. The anomaly was detected and the attached source records should be checked before the irreversible action is taken.

context.action_boundary: May facilities approve an emergency repair invoice using a delegated approver after the primary approver is unavailable?
context.anomaly_observed: Approval uses a delegated approver instead of the primary approver.
context.explanation_summary: Operations note is plausible but not the controlling delegation record.

internal_documents.content: Invoice HMS-2026-0816 covers emergency chiller repair at site FAC-22.
Emergency work order EWO-HMS-016 covers chiller restoration at site FAC-22.
Approval uses delegated approver D-447 instead of the primary approver.
Delegation register DEL-HMS-016 is active for site FAC-18 only and routine maintenance only; it does not cover site FAC-22 emergency-work order approval before invoice approval.
Service acceptance record SAR-HMS-016 confirms chiller repair completion.
Facilities approval log records delegated approver D-447 and primary approver absence.
A delegated-approval anomaly can be resolved only by an active delegation register that covers site, category, amount band, and work type before approval.
Operations note says the delegated approver usually covers emergency facilities work.

policy_documents.title: Explained anomaly review standard
policy_documents.content: A real anomaly can be closed only by source records that match the anomaly scope and timing before the action. Plausible notes or informal explanations are not enough by themselves.
